industries.name: Compound Semiconductors & Electronics
uses: Gallium arsenide (GaAs) and gallium nitride (GaN) are III-V compound semiconductors with electron mobility far exceeding silicon — GaAs used in solar cells, RF amplifiers, and laser diodes; GaN dominates high-frequency, high-power RF and power electronics

GaN-on-silicon (GaN-Si) power transistors are replacing silicon MOSFETs in EV onboard chargers, data center power supplies, and consumer fast chargers (65W–240W GaN chargers) — GaN switches faster with lower losses at the same voltage rating

GaN-on-SiC high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) for defense radar, 5G base station power amplifiers, and satellite communications — handles 10× more power per unit area than GaAs at frequencies above 10 GHz

GaAs multi-junction solar cells achieve 47%+ efficiency under concentrated sunlight — used in satellite power systems where weight and area are constrained; terrestrial CPV (concentrating PV) installations
Gallium indium arsenide (InGaAs) photodetectors for fiber optic receivers, night vision, and LiDAR sensors — detects 1300–1600nm telecom wavelengths invisible to silicon detectors

companies.name: Wolfspeed (formerly Cree)
companies.use: World's largest SiC and GaN-on-SiC wafer manufacturer. Produces GaN HEMT devices for 5G base stations, defense radar, and EV power electronics. Its Mohawk Valley fab is the world's largest 200mm SiC wafer fab.

companies.name: Infineon Technologies
companies.use: Major GaN-on-Si power device manufacturer. Produces CoolGaN transistors for EV charging, telecom, and industrial power supplies. Also major SiC MOSFET producer for EV inverters.

companies.name: Qorvo
companies.use: Leading GaN-on-SiC RF device manufacturer for 5G infrastructure, defense radar (AESA arrays), and satellite communications. Supplies GaN power amplifiers to Ericsson, Nokia, and defense prime contractors.

companies.name: China Hongqiao / Chinalco
companies.use: China dominates global gallium production — gallium is a byproduct of aluminum refining from bauxite (Bayer process liquor). China's export restrictions on gallium announced August 2023 created significant supply chain concern in Western semiconductor industries.

industries.name: LEDs & Solid-State Lighting
uses: Gallium nitride (GaN) blue LEDs — the 2014 Nobel Prize-winning invention by Akasaki, Amano, and Nakamura. Blue GaN LEDs combined with yellow phosphor produce white light, enabling the LED lighting revolution that displaced incandescent and fluorescent lamps globally
InGaN (indium gallium nitride) quantum well active layers in blue and green LEDs — indium content tunes emission wavelength from 365nm (UV) to 520nm (green)

GaN micro-LEDs for next-generation displays: Apple Watch Ultra, future AR/VR headsets, and direct-view microLED TVs use arrays of GaN micro-LEDs smaller than 100 microns for ultra-high brightness and efficiency
GaN-based violet laser diodes (405nm) for Blu-ray disc players and high-power laser projectors
AlGaInP (aluminum gallium indium phosphide) red and amber LEDs for automotive tail lights, traffic signals, and full-color outdoor displays

companies.name: Nichia Corporation
companies.use: World's largest LED manufacturer and inventor of the first high-brightness GaN blue LED (Shuji Nakamura, 1993). Produces GaN LEDs for general lighting, automotive, and display backlighting. Closely guards GaN epitaxy technology.

companies.name: ams-Osram
companies.use: Major LED manufacturer producing GaN-based LEDs for automotive headlights, consumer lighting, horticultural lighting, and specialty applications. Developing micro-LED technology for AR/VR displays.

companies.name: Seoul Semiconductor
companies.use: Major GaN LED manufacturer specializing in UV LEDs for disinfection, horticulture LEDs, and automotive lighting. Its SunLike series mimics natural sunlight spectrum for human-centric lighting applications.

industries.name: Medical Imaging & Research
uses: Gallium-67 (⁶⁷Ga) citrate as nuclear medicine tracer for tumor imaging, infection/inflammation detection, and lymphoma staging — gamma camera imaging of gallium uptake in rapidly dividing cells
Gallium-68 (⁶⁸Ga) PET imaging agent: ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE for neuroendocrine tumor detection; ⁶⁸Ga-PSMA for prostate cancer staging — superior to CT and MRI for detecting small metastases

Gallium maltolate and gallium nitrate as antimicrobial and anticancer investigational drugs — gallium mimics iron in biological systems, disrupting iron-dependent bacterial metabolism and cancer cell proliferation
Galinstan (Ga-In-Sn liquid metal alloy, melting point -19°C) as non-toxic replacement for mercury in thermometers, pressure sensors, and flexible electronics

companies.name: Isotopia Molecular Imaging
companies.use: Producer of gallium-68 radiopharmaceuticals for PET imaging of neuroendocrine tumors and prostate cancer. Supplies ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE and ⁶⁸Ga-PSMA to nuclear medicine centers across Europe and Israel.

companies.name: Advanced Accelerator Applications (Novartis)
companies.use: Major radiopharmaceutical company producing Lutathera (¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE therapy) and developing gallium-68 PSMA diagnostic agents for prostate cancer. Supplies ⁶⁸Ga tracers to PET centers globally.
